zone_6b_notes: Direct sow in early spring (late March to April) or fall in Farmington. Dill dislikes transplanting â€” sow where it will grow. Bolts in summer heat; sow successive crops every 3 weeks through May for continuous foliage harvest. For seed harvest (pickling), let plants flower and go to seed â€” harvest heads when seeds begin to brown. Dill self-seeds readily and will naturalize in a garden bed. Excellent trap crop for swallowtail butterfly caterpillars (parsleyworm). Keep away from carrots and fennel â€” crosses readily. Ideal companion for cucumbers and tomatoes.

planting.best_planting_time: Direct sow late March through May; succession sow every 3 weeks

water: moderate â€” 1 inch/week; tolerates some drought once established
